name: Chicken Jambalaya 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon over medium-low heat until crisp, about 10 minutes.
2. Remove and set aside.
3. Mix flour with chili powder and cayenne pepper.
4. Sprinkle chicken with salt and black pepper; dredge in seasoned flour to coat (reserve remaining flour).
5. Cook chicken in the Dutch oven over medium heat, turning after 15 minutes, or until brown on all sides.
6. Remove chicken; keep warm.
7. Drain off all but 4 tablespoons drippings in the pan; add reserved flour mixture and cook over low heat, stirring until dark brown, about 8 minutes.
8. Add onion, celery, bell pepper, and garlic; continue to stir for 5 minutes (vegetables will remain slightly crisp).
9. Measure liquid from tomatoes and add enough water to make 1 cup; chop tomatoes and add to the Dutch oven.
10. Slowly stir in chicken broth, tomato liquid, water, rice, salt, and chili powder into the mixture.
11. Return chicken to the pan; add Worcestershire sauce and bay leaf.
12. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to lowest setting, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes, or until rice is done.
13. Sprinkle with crumbled bacon before serving.
